target.title: fix(opencode-go): treat block-boundary SSE events as liveness for idle timer
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Treat block-boundary SSE events (`text_end`, `thinking_end`,
`toolcall_start`, `toolcall_end`) as liveness signals that reset the
idle timer, preventing premature stream abort when the model pauses at
block boundaries.

## Changes

- `extensions/opencode-go/stream-termination.ts`: Add 4 block-boundary
  event types to `isProviderProgressEvent` so the idle timeout is reset
  at block transitions.

## Evidence

Before: Block-boundary SSE events did not reset the idle timer, causing
premature abort on slow streams with quiet block transitions.

After: The idle timer is reset at `text_end`, `thinking_end`,
`toolcall_start`, and `toolcall_end` events, so the stream survives
normal block-boundary pauses.

_AI-assisted._

target.title: fix(memory): backfill provider.model in createWithAdapter when adapter returns empty string
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Backfill `provider.model` when the adapter returns an empty string so callers
always see the resolved model name. An empty model string during boot can
overwrite a correct index identity and leave the search index permanently dirty.

## Changes

- Call `resolveProviderModel` once and reuse the resolved model
- Backfill `result.provider.model` with `resolvedModel` when adapter returns
  an empty string: `result.provider ? { ...result.provider, model: result.provider.model || resolvedModel } : null`

## Evidence

### Before (empty model → dirty index)
```
provider.model = ""  // adapter returned empty string, index identity was lost
```

### After (model backfilled from resolved default)
```
provider.model = "text-embedding-3-small"  // resolved from adapter.defaultModel
```

_AI-assisted._

target.title: fix(vydra,xai,runway,pixverse,deepinfra,together,elevenlabs,microsoft): bound JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Replace unbounded `await response.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` (1 MiB cap)
across  bound JSON response reads to prevent OOM:8 extensions, preventing OOM from oversized upstream HTTP responses.

## Evidence

### Real HTTP bounded-read proof

```
node --import tsx test/_proof_bounded_json.mts

[proof] real server on :58033, cap=1048576 bytes, would-stream≈67108864 bytes
[proof] small server on :58034

PASS  oversized body: throws bounded cap error :: threw=true msg="Error: JSON response exceeds 1048576 bytes"
PASS  stream cancelled at cap: server sent ≈ cap bytes, not ~64 MiB :: bytesSent=1048576 (cap=1048576, would-stream=67108864)
PASS  happy path: small JSON parsed correctly :: status=ok id=batch-1
PASS  negative control: unbounded read buffers PAST cap :: buffered=33554457 bytes (> 1048576)

[proof] 4 PASS, 0 FAIL
```

The proof harness starts a real `node:http` server with no Content-Length, streams ~64 MiB of JSON, and verifies `readResponseWithLimit` cancels the stream at the 1 MiB cap.

_AI-assisted._

target.title: fix(agent): surface non_deliverable_terminal_turn with a specific retry message
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Surfaces `non_deliverable_terminal_turn` with a specific retry message instead
of the generic "Agent couldn't generate a response" fallback. The marker was
already classified but never surfaced to users.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts` | Add `terminalError` to `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult` type |
| `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts` | Add `terminalError` to `EmbeddedAgentRunMeta` type |
| `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` | Propagate `terminalError` from attemptTrajectoryTerminal |
| `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` | Spread `terminalError` into incomplete_turn meta blocks |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-lifecycle-terminal.ts` | Add `terminalError` to deferred terminal metadata keys |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-failure-copy.ts` | Add `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForTerminalError` + constants |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` | Wire `terminalError` into `buildExternalRunFailureReply` |

## Evidence

### Unit tests: terminal error propagation + fallback copy routing

```
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-trajectory-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  233 passed (233)
   Duration  98.06s
```

### Retry-first copy

When the model stops between tool calls without producing a reply:
> ⚠️ The model stopped between tool calls without producing a reply. Please
> try the same message again — this usually resolves on retry. If it
> persists, use /new or a lighter model.

instead of the old generic fallback.

_AI-assisted._

target.title: fix: same-session inferred commitments can be starved indefinitely when a normal HEARTBEAT.md...
target.body:
## Summary

Found one regression risk: same-session inferred commitments can be starved indefinitely when a normal `HEARTBEAT.md` task is due on every heartbeat tick.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Medium: recurring due heartbeat tasks can prevent same-session commitments from ever delivering** (regression)
  - File: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:1024`
  - Evidence: `firstDueCommitment` is now only selected when `dueHeartbeatTasks.length === 0` (`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:1024`). When any task is due, `resolveHeartbeatRunPrompt` returns the task prompt with `hasDueCommitments: false` (`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:870`), so `dueCommitmentIds` is empty and the commitment is neither attempted nor delivered. The scheduler’s follow-up pass explicitly skips the default session key (`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:1866`), so same-session commitments depend on a later default heartbeat run with no due tasks. For a documented/common setup like `heartbeat.every: "5m"` and a `tasks:` entry with `interval: 5m`, `isTaskDue` is true again on every cadence tick because it uses `nowMs - lastRunMs >= intervalMs` (`src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts:288`). The new test covers the first half of this state by asserting the due commitment stays `pending` with `attempts...
  - Impact: users with opt-in inferred commitments and an every-heartbeat task can stop receiving natural follow-up check-ins for that session until the commitment expires, despite docs saying due commitments are delivered through heartbeat for the same agent/channel scope.
  - Suggested fix: keep task turns tool-capable, but process same-session commitments in a separate no-tools turn after the task run, or add a commitment-only wake/path that bypasses task selection without mixing untrusted commitment metadata into the tool-capable task prompt.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`
- `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/955a0e9c0f7d969e7d96cade7b383b3306aadf98.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/955a0e9c0f7d969e7d96cade7b383b3306aadf98
- Latest main at intake: 7340c0322fc3f7b1279729663f3ae37bd4bf978f
- Original commit author: clawsweeper[bot]
- GitHub author: @clawsweeper[bot]
- Highest severity: medium
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `173f959613b35195c8e4e662b9e0138831cfb4d2..955a0e9c0f7d969e7d96cade7b383b3306aadf98`
- Changed files: `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`, `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts`
- Code read: changed files in full, task parsing/due logic, commitment store selection/status updates, heartbeat wake/scheduler loop, heartbeat and commitment docs.
- PR metadata: associated PR `75344` reviewed via `gh`.

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm docs:list` completed.
- `git diff --check 173f959613b35195c8e4e662b9e0138831cfb4d2..955a0e9c0f7d969e7d96cade7b383b3306aadf98` passed.
- Initial focused test run failed because `node_modules` was missing; `pnpm install` succeeded.
- `pnpm test src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` passed: 41 tests.

Known review limits:
- I did not run the broad changed gate.
- An inline `tsx` smoke attempt was stopped because importing the runner staged bundled plugin runtime dependencies and did not reach scenario output quickly enough.

## ClawSweeper Guardrails

- Re-check the finding against latest `main` before changing code.
- Keep the patch to the narrowest behavior change and matching regression coverage.
- Do not merge automatically; this PR stays for maintainer review.

## Summary

Fixes the Windows `openclaw doctor` shell-completion cache path observed in the maintainer workshop: doctor was spawning `openclaw completion --write-state` without the plugin-command skip guard, so completion cache generation could load plugin CLI commands and trigger bundled runtime dependency work during doctor.

The update path already sets `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_SKIP_PLUGIN_COMMANDS=1` for this same cache write. This applies the same guard to doctor-triggered completion cache writes.

- `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts`

target.title: docs(capabilities): define workspace discovery convention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: workspace-local integrations and durable procedures can be documented ad hoc, so agents may miss valid local capability paths before saying a task is unsupported.
- Why it matters: users with bridge queues, taskflow recipes, ACP delegation paths, or local integration wrappers need a consistent way to make those capabilities discoverable across sessions.
- What changed: added a docs-only workspace capability descriptor convention under `capabilities/*.md`, linked it from workspace/bootstrap docs, and documented current bootstrap-extra-files limits.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no runtime auto-discovery, gateway wrapper, daemon, auth layer, session store, or tool policy change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #76074
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Docs only. No runtime behavior change.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Read `docs/concepts/workspace-capabilities.md`.
2. Confirm `docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md` links the optional `capabilities/` workspace folder.
3. Confirm `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md` and `docs/automation/hooks.md` state that descriptors are not part of the fixed bootstrap set by default.

### Expected

- The docs describe a narrow workspace capability descriptor convention without implying runtime permission or gateway behavior changes.

### Actual

- Matches expected.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Commands:

```bash
pnpm docs:list
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/concepts/workspace-capabilities.md docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md docs/concepts/system-prompt.md docs/automation/hooks.md docs/docs.json
pnpm docs:check-mdx
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: docs list command, whitespace check, docs formatting check for changed docs, MDX docs check.
- Edge cases checked: new docs page is listed in `docs/docs.json`; docs explicitly avoid gateway wrapper/proxy/daemon behavior.
- What you did **not** verify: rendered docs site in browser.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: workspace capability descriptors are documented as a discoverable, docs-only convention without implying new runtime permissions or Gateway behavior.
- Real environment tested: local Linux checkout of `openclaw-src` after rebasing this PR branch onto `upstream/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `DOCS_I18N_GLOSSARY_BASE=upstream/main pnpm check:docs`
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the real local checkout:

```text
Docs formatting clean (604 files).
Summary: 0 error(s)
Docs MDX check passed (619 files, 4862ms).
checked_internal_links=4218
broken_links=0
```

- Observed result after fix: the docs verification completed successfully and the link audit reported `broken_links=0`.
- What was not tested: rendered docs site in a browser.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: readers may assume descriptors are automatically loaded into every prompt.
  - Mitigation: the docs explicitly state descriptors are not part of the fixed bootstrap set by default and should be linked intentionally or surfaced via hooks.

target.title: fix(memory): improve QMD recall for channel queries
target.body:
## Summary

- allow QMD recall for Discord channel sessions in the default scope
- retry empty/noisy QMD search queries once with a tighter lexical query built from priority memory anchors
- report scope-denied QMD searches in debug output instead of making them indistinguishable from true zero-hit searches

## Why

Gary's live Samantha tests in Discord showed `memory_search` using QMD but returning zero hits for a noisy memory query. Local log inspection showed the first failure mode was QMD scope denial for Discord channel sessions. After allowing channel sessions, the intentionally noisy query could reach QMD, but needed a conservative lexical retry to recover useful memory hits rather than stopping at an empty result.

This stays separate from #75761, which handles active-memory timeout behavior.

## Validation

- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- live Samantha Discord test: the noisy query `thread memory retrieval active-memory QMD semantic memory search Discord test` returned 4 QMD hits with `fallback: relaxed-lexical-zero-hit`, and Samantha was able to follow with `memory_get`

target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: `/subagents list` and related subagent control paths could miss runs when the requester was represented by a main-session alias such as `main` versus `agent:<agentId>:main`.
  - Why it matters: Users could spawn subagents successfully but fail to list or control them from the main session, making background work appear lost.
  - What changed: Canonicalized main-session requester/controller keys at the subagent list/ control boundaries and added regression coverage for both tool and slash-command paths.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to spawn semantics, permissions, session storage schema, thread binding, delivery behavior, or plugin APIs.

target.title: fix(gateway): make `gateway start` idempotent when already running
target.body:
Fixes #60087.

## Summary

If you have `openclaw gateway start` in your `~/.profile` (the setup hint adds it), every exec tool call ends up SIGTERM'ing the running gateway and dropping your WebSocket session.

The chain:

```
exec tool → bash -l -c "env -0" (PATH probe)
         → ~/.profile sources → openclaw gateway start
         → systemctl restart → SIGTERM
```

`gateway start` was unconditionally calling the platform restart action. It now early-returns when the service is already running.

`start` means "ensure running". Use `openclaw gateway restart` if you want to force a cycle.

## Changes

- New `"already-running"` outcome on `GatewayServiceStartResult`.
- `startGatewayService` skips `service.restart()` when `state.running` is true.
- CLI emits a friendly "service is already running" message instead of pretending it restarted.
- Fixed two existing lifecycle tests that were named like "stopped service" / "scheduled restart" scenarios but never overrode the default `running` mock — they passed only because the old code path was unconditional.

Credit to @Slybry2000 for the report and trace.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/daemon/service.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` — all pass
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:test:src`, `oxfmt --check` clean
- [ ] `pnpm check:changed` in Testbox before merge

## Related (not duplicates)

This is a distinct mechanism from the prior cluster of "exec triggers gateway SIGTERM" fixes. Each addressed a different layer:

- #62086 (closed) — Docker `OPENCLAW_SHELL=exec` runtime-wrapper SIGTERM path (the second mechanism @amnesia106 reported in the issue thread).
- #32348 (closed) — Linux/systemd restart-loop angle when restart was issued from inside an agent session.
- #41198, #41752, #41815, #41978 (all closed) — macOS LaunchAgent restart semantics (kickstart/bootout/auto-restart).
- #30083, #40799, #11187 (all closed) — earlier exec-triggered SIGTERM paths now resolved.

This PR closes the remaining gap: making `startGatewayService()` itself idempotent so the `~/.profile` re-invocation doesn't trigger a platform restart in the first place. Complementary to the above, not duplicative.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60087

target.title: fix(validation): preserve null in anyOf unions instead of coercing to empty string (fixes #96716)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96716.

MCP tool calls with optional arguments set to JSON null fail via OpenClaw but succeed via other MCP clients. Root cause: when a JSON schema uses `anyOf [{type: string}, {type: null}]`, the union coercion (`coerceWithUnionSchema`) tries the string branch first, which coerces `null` to `""` (a valid string), and returns that before ever trying the `{type: null}` branch. The MCP server receives `""` instead of `null`, triggering a different code path.

## Why This Change Was Made

`coerceWithUnionSchema` iterated schemas in order and applied `coerceWithJsonSchema` (which uses `coercePrimitiveByType`) before validating. For `anyOf [{type: string}, {type: null}]` with value `null`, the first schema coerces `null` to `""` and the validator passes — so the null branch is never tried.

The fix adds a **first pass that prefers exact type match over coercion**. If `null` exactly matches `{type: null}`, return the original null value directly without attempting coercion.

## Changes Made

- `packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts`: Added exact-type-match first pass in `coerceWithUnionSchema`.
- `packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts`: Added regression test verifying null is preserved for `anyOf [{type: string}, {type: null}]`.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts` — 3/3 passed

## Real behavior proof

**Environment:** Linux, OpenClaw 2026.6.10, Node 24.13.1

**Proof 1: null preserved for anyOf [string, null] (issue #96716 scenario)**

```
INPUT:  {"insight_id":null,"cluster_name":"testenv-cluster"}
OUTPUT: {"insight_id":null,"cluster_name":"testenv-cluster"}
insight_id === null: true
insight_id === '':  false
✅ null preserved — not coerced to empty string
```

**Proof 2: normal string values still work (no regression)**

```
STRING INPUT -> OUTPUT: {"insight_id":"eks-123","cluster_name":"testenv"}
✅ String values pass through unchanged
```

**Proof 3: non-nullable type coercion still works (no regression)**

```
COERCION INPUT 1e3 -> OUTPUT: {"amount":1000}
✅ Numeric coercion still works for non-nullable schemas
```

- ✅ Null values in `anyOf [string, null]` schemas preserved as null
- ✅ Normal string values unchanged
- ✅ Non-nullable type coercion (number, integer) still works
- ✅ No impact on non-union schemas

**What was not tested:** End-to-end with a real MCP server (the fix is in the shared validation layer — all tool calls benefit from it).

- [x] AI-assisted

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix

- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.

target.title: fix: making typing start fire-and-forget allows cleanup/idle to run before a persistent typin...
target.body:
## Summary

Found one concrete regression: making typing start fire-and-forget allows cleanup/idle to run before a persistent typing indicator has finished starting, so the stop path can no-op and leave the indicator behind.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Medium: Pending typing start can outlive cleanup and leave persistent indicators stuck** (regression)
  - File: `src/channels/typing.ts:79`
  - Evidence: `onReplyStart` now starts `fireStart()` in the background and returns after one microtask at `src/channels/typing.ts:79-87`, while `fireStop` immediately marks the callbacks closed and calls `stop` at `src/channels/typing.ts:90-98`. For Feishu, `start` assigns `typingState` only after `await addTypingIndicator(...)` at `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts:176`; `stop` returns without doing anything if `typingState` is still unset at `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts:183-185`. A fast cleanup while `addTypingIndicator` is still in flight therefore calls stop too early, then the delayed start completes and records a persistent reaction with no later removal.
  - Impact: Feishu typing reactions are explicitly persistent until removed. A quick reply, `NO_REPLY`, empty-message cleanup, cancellation, or send-policy path can leave a visible typing reaction/indicator on the parent message after the run is already finished.
  - Suggested fix: keep typing start off the reply critical path, but serialize cleanup against the pending start. For example, track the current start promise and, if stop/cleanup happens while it is pending, run `stop` again after the promise settles when a start actually completed. Alternatively make persistent-indicator channels expose an abortable/pending-aware start helper.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `src/channels/typing.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/typing.ts`
- `src/channels/typing.test.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/45b86450795d8bd3d1e548c8815cd97d6583199d.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/45b86450795d8bd3d1e548c8815cd97d6583199d
- Latest main at intake: 8093ae60292f4d9009b3d48f234b39b5cbc538e5
- Original commit author: Ayaan Zaidi
- GitHub author: @obviyus
- Highest severity: medium
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `40b0b1bfe051072f47d0160eb078afa3b17cef48..45b86450795d8bd3d1e548c8815cd97d6583199d`
- Changed files: `src/auto-reply/reply/typing.ts`, `src/channels/typing.ts`, `src/channels/typing.test.ts`
- Code read: changed files in full; `src/channels/typing-lifecycle.ts`; `src/channels/typing-start-guard.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-dispatcher.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts`; `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`; relevant channel typing call sites, including Feishu, Matrix, Telegram, Discord, Signal, Mattermost, MSTeams, Zalo, and heartbeat typing.
- Dependencies/web: no dependency files changed; no web lookup needed.

## Expected validation

target.title: Gateway: guard session-run cancel requester in minimal tests
target.body:
## Summary
- Rebased this replacement lane onto latest `origin/main` and kept the minimal-test-gateway requester guard from the prior patch.
- Bounded sticky session-run cancel replay state with a 10 minute TTL plus a 1,000-entry cap, while preserving late-handler replay semantics.
- Added a production cleanup path: registered chat/agent run teardown now clears the matching sticky cancel target after the owning run finishes/aborts.
- Documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime` in `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`, including supported imports (`onSessionRunCancel`, `requestSessionRunCancel`, public types) and the explicit note that the core-owned emitter is not public.
- Added the required `CHANGELOG.md` Unreleased entry for the Plugin SDK import path and Gateway cancel behavior.

## Integration note
- #75371 remains the clean replacement lane for #68624. It no longer carries the earlier stacked `sessions_send` scope from #68622.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Plugin-owned delegated tasks can use `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime` to request cancellation of the owning Gateway session run; Gateway aborts the run, emits the core-owned cancel to plugin observers, preserves sticky replay briefly for late registration, and clears sticky replay during run cleanup. The core-owned emitter remains unavailable through the public plugin SDK subpath.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `f9343ffcda` on Node `v22.22.2` in the repository worktree, using the real in-process Gateway chat-abort runtime (`registerChatAbortController` + `wireSessionRunCancelRequester`) and the plugin-facing session-run-cancel runtime import path. No external network sends or secrets were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a one-off local runtime proof script with `node --import tsx <proof-script>.mts`. The script registered a real Gateway chat abort controller, wired `wireSessionRunCancelRequester(...)`, imported `onSessionRunCancel` and `requestSessionRunCancel` through the plugin SDK runtime source path, requested cancel for `{ kind:"session_run", sessionKey:"agent:proof:main", runId:"proof-run-1" }`, then checked the Gateway abort signal, plugin observer callback, sticky replay before cleanup, and cleanup state after `active.cleanup()`.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the local proof command:

```json
{
  "proof": "local gateway chat-abort runtime + public plugin SDK session-run-cancel-runtime",
  "publicPluginImport": "openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime",
  "coreEmitterPublic": false,
  "requestResult": { "requested": true, "aborted": true },
  "abortSignalAborted": true,
  "pluginObservedCancel": {
    "target": { "kind": "session_run", "sessionKey": "agent:proof:main", "runId": "proof-run-1" },
    "reason": { "source": "chat-abort", "message": "real runtime proof" }
  },
  "stickyReplayBeforeRunCleanup": { "source": "chat-abort", "message": "real runtime proof" },
  "stickyReplayAfterRunCleanupCount": 0,
  "terminalStateAfterRunCleanup": false,
  "abortedBroadcast": { "runId": "proof-run-1", "sessionKey": "agent:proof:main", "seq": 5, "state": "aborted", "stopReason": "real runtime proof" },
  "nodeSessionEventCount": 1,
  "boundedTerminalMaxEntries": 1000,
  "boundedTerminalTtlMs": 600000
}
```

Observed result after fix: The plugin-side cancel request returned `{ requested:true, aborted:true }`; the Gateway AbortController signal was aborted; the plugin observer saw exactly one `{ source:"chat-abort" }` cancellation; late registration replayed the sticky cancel before run cleanup; run cleanup removed sticky replay (`stickyReplayAfterRunCleanupCount:0`, `terminalStateAfterRunCleanup:false`); and the Gateway emitted an aborted chat payload with the requested stop reason.

What was not tested: External channel delivery, remote production Gateway deployment, and third-party plugin package publishing were not exercised in this local proof.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/sessions/session-run-cancel.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-early.test.ts` → pass (6 files, 52 tests across configured projects)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts --testNamePattern "smoke: supports abort and idempotent completion"` → pass
- `pnpm run plugin-sdk:api:gen && pnpm run plugin-sdk:api:check` → pass (`OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`)
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported` → pass

- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` → pass
- `pnpm run lint:core` → pass
- `pnpm run build` → pass

target.title: fix(whatsapp): Add group admin privacy controls
target.body:
Closes #69233

## What changed

This adds an optional per-group WhatsApp admin override via `channels.whatsapp.groups.<jid>.admin` and wildcard `channels.whatsapp.groups."*".admin`.

When an admin is configured for a group scope:
- the configured E.164 number bypasses mention gating for that group
- non-admin group members cannot run slash commands in that group
- the configured admin remains able to run slash commands for that group

When no admin is configured, the existing group command authorization behavior is preserved.

## Why

The issue described a privacy gap in mixed-trust WhatsApp groups: non-admin members could still probe bot behavior through mentions and slash commands. The first pass of this feature covered the basic config and enforcement, but upstream readiness also needed the behavior pinned down for wildcard group config and for the no-admin-configured fallback path.

## User impact

Users can now designate a WhatsApp group admin by phone number and use that as a privacy boundary for command discovery in group chats.

This is intentionally backwards-compatible:
- groups without `admin` continue using the existing command authorization rules
- exact-group and wildcard group config both work
- docs now describe the new config key

## Root cause

OpenClaw already had group policy, allowlists, and mention gating, but it did not have a per-group owner/admin concept for WhatsApp. That meant there was no narrow way to let a trusted operator manage the bot in a group without exposing command access to other participants covered by broader allowlist rules.

## Validation

- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/group-policy.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- test extensions/whatsapp/src`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- tsgo:core`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- tsgo:extensions`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- check`
- `npm exec pnpm@10.9.0 -- build`

## AI assistance

- AI-assisted: yes, with Codex
- Testing: fully tested locally with the commands listed above
- Bot review threads addressed and resolved after follow-up fixes

target.title: fix(ui): improve Thai locale copy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the new Thai Control UI locale still had high-visible English fallback copy and a few unnatural Thai labels around common controls, the agent detail surface, files, channels, cron panels, and the language picker.
- Why it matters: Thai users see these strings immediately after selecting Thai, and the docs/UI translation pipeline needs one shared terminology style.
- What changed: added Thai UI/docs glossary terms, updated Thai translation memory, regenerated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts`, added a Thai copy regression test, and fixed the i18n generator process decoder so formatted multibyte locale output does not get replacement characters when stdout chunks split UTF-8 bytes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no locale registry changes, no supported-locale changes, no docs generated-output edits under `docs/th/**`, no Mintlify/navigation behavior changes, and no public API or runtime contract changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #70510
- Related #3460
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Thai was recently enabled in the Control UI picker, but the Thai translation memory still carried English fallbacks and weaker terminology for high-visible agent/file/channel labels. While regenerating, the formatter subprocess output path could also decode UTF-8 chunks with `String(chunk)`, which can corrupt Thai text if a multibyte character is split across chunks.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing i18n tests checked structural alignment and non-English fallback health, but did not lock specific Thai high-visible labels or replacement-character regressions.
- Contributing context (if known): #70510 tracks broader Thai docs feedback, so this PR intentionally updates glossary sources and Control UI copy without editing generated docs output.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Thai common, language picker, agent, file preview, and cron delivery copy stays localized and does not contain the prior replacement-character corruption.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these keys are generated output consumed directly by the Control UI, so a locale-level unit assertion catches both TM drift and generator corruption.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): structural locale alignment already existed in the same file, but it did not assert Thai wording quality.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Thai Control UI copy is more natural for high-visible labels such as Agent, Workspace, Tools, Channel, Default, Preview, language names, and file preview controls. Future Thai docs/UI translation runs also get the updated mixed technical glossary.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [mixed English fallbacks and awkward labels]

After:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [Thai localized labels using shared glossary terms]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v24.13.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI i18n
- Relevant config (redacted): no translation provider configured; existing TM cache used

### Steps

1. Regenerate Thai Control UI locale from source translation memory.
2. Run i18n drift, targeted locale tests, docs glossary checks, formatter checks, and changed gate.
3. Inspect the generated Thai bundle for replacement-character corruption.

### Expected

- Thai locale bundle stays generator-clean, high-visible Thai labels are localized, and glossary source files stay valid.

### Actual

- Expected behavior confirmed locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run:

```text
node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts sync --locale th --write
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed
```

Notes:

- `pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest shards: 81 UI files / 786 UI tests and 1 unit-fast file / 3 tests.
- `pnpm check:changed` selected core, coreTests, docs, and tooling lanes and completed successfully.
- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because the local `blacksmith` CLI is not installed. The unpinned remote installer was not executed; the local changed gate above was used as the fallback.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: regenerated Thai locale from TM, verified `ui:i18n:check` clean for all locales, verified Thai high-visible copy assertions, verified docs Thai glossary source check, verified no replacement characters remain in the Thai locale/TM, and verified local changed gate.
- Edge cases checked: UTF-8 multibyte formatter output from the i18n generator, language picker Thai/English labels, agent workspace/file preview labels, and cron delivery summary label.
- What you did **not** verify: browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the Blacksmith CLI was unavailable locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Thai wording preferences can be subjective.
  - Mitigation: kept the scope to high-visible UI labels and encoded the mixed technical style in both UI and docs glossaries so future translation passes stay consistent.
- Risk: generator script change touches shared tooling.
  - Mitigation: narrowed the change to UTF-8 stream decoding for subprocess output and covered the observed Thai replacement-character failure with a locale test plus `ui:i18n:check`.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Thai Control UI high-visible labels now load from the regenerated Thai locale bundle with localized agent, workspace, channel, file-preview, and language picker copy instead of the prior English/awkward fallback wording.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.4.1 with Node v24.13.0 and pnpm 10.33.2, using the generated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts` bundle after the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the generated Thai Control UI locale bundle with Node/tsx and printed the labels a Thai user would see in the agent/file/language picker surfaces.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from a live `node` locale import in this OpenClaw checkout:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { th } from \"./ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts\"; const proof = { commonDefault: th.agents.default, agentTabsTools: th.agents.tabs.tools, agentChannelsTitle: th.agents.channels.title, agentWorkspace: th.agents.context.workspace, filePreview: th.agents.files.preview, fileEmptyDraft: th.agents.files.emptyDraft, thaiLanguageLabel: th.languages.th }; console.log(JSON.stringify(proof, null, 2));"
{
  "commonDefault": "ค่าเริ่มต้น",
  "agentTabsTools": "เครื่องมือ",
  "agentChannelsTitle": "ช่องทาง",
  "agentWorkspace": "เวิร์กสเปซ",
  "filePreview": "ดูตัวอย่าง",
  "fileEmptyDraft": "ร่างว่าง",
  "thaiLanguageLabel": "ไทย"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The generated Thai bundle returns localized Thai strings for the visible Agent/Tools/Channel/Workspace/File Preview/Thai language labels, with no replacement-character corruption in the sampled output.
- **What was not tested**: Browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the local `blacksmith` and `crabbox` CLIs are not installed in this environment.

target.title: fix: macOS-only Parallels smoke harness regression
target.body:
## Summary

Found one macOS-only Parallels smoke harness regression. The new guest script writer creates a root-owned `700` script, then the macOS lane executes it as the desktop user.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Low: macOS update script is not readable by the user that executes it** (reliability)
  - File: `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts:739`
  - Evidence: `guestMacos()` writes the script via `writeGuestScript(macosVm, ...)`, then runs it through `prlctl exec ... --current-user ... /bin/bash <scriptPath>` or the sudo desktop-user fallback at lines 451-453. But `writeGuestScript()` writes with plain `prlctl exec <vm> /usr/bin/tee <scriptPath>` and then `chmod 700 <scriptPath>` at lines 730-739. The Parallels docs describe `--current-user` as logging in as the current guest OS user, so the write path and execute path are different identities for the macOS lane. ([docs.parallels.com](https://docs.parallels.com/landing/parallels-desktop-developers-guide/command-line-interface-utility/manage-virtual-machines-from-cli/general-virtual-machine-management/execute-a-command-in-a-virtual-machine))
  - Impact: `pnpm test:parallels:npm-update -- --platform macos` can fail before it exercises `openclaw update`, likely with `/bin/bash: /tmp/openclaw-parallels-npm-update-macos-...sh: Permission denied`. That breaks the intended release/update smoke coverage on current `main`; no later commit in the touched files fixes it.
  - Suggested fix: for macOS, write/chmod the temp script through the same resolved transport used to execute it, or explicitly `chown` it to the resolved desktop user and keep permissions non-world-readable because the script embeds provider credentials. Keep the Linux root-owned path separate.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`
- `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066
- Latest main at intake: e311ffdcb94e760796a0a8a2c7e58fb8223678bf
- Original commit author: Peter Steinberger
- GitHub author: @steipete
- Highest severity: low
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `231e5c618f8ae700d51d080e4182a57588da888b..af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066`
- Changed files: `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts`, `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`
- Code read: full changed files, `scripts/AGENTS.md`, Parallels smoke skill, adjacent guest transport helpers, macOS/Linux smoke helpers, package script entrypoint
- Dependencies/web: official Parallels `prlctl exec` docs; no package dependency changes
- Commands: `pnpm docs:list`, `git show`, `git diff`, `git diff --check`, `pnpm install`, `pnpm exec tsx scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts --help`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts --help`: passed after installing deps
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`: passed
- `git diff --check ...`: passed

Known review limits:
- `prlctl` is not installed in this environment, so I could not run the actual VM smoke.

## ClawSweeper Guardrails

- Re-check the finding against latest `main` before changing code.
- Keep the patch to the narrowest behavior change and matching regression coverage.
- Do not merge automatically; this PR stays for maintainer review.

target.title: fix(agent): prevent sticky model fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- roll back temporary auto fallback model overrides after successful fallback attempts
- keep session routing pointed at the selected model while still recording usage/cost for the actual fallback model used
- update runner/session accounting coverage and add the Unreleased changelog entry

## Root cause
Successful fallback candidates were persisted into session override state but only rolled back on errors/classified failures. Usage persistence also wrote `providerUsed`/`modelUsed` into the session identity, so one fallback run could pin later turns to the smaller fallback model even after the primary model recovered.

## Validation
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A temporary model fallback should not make the session stick to the fallback model. After a primary-model rate limit sends one turn to a configured fallback, the next turn in the same session should try the configured primary again while usage/status still records the actual fallback runtime used for the fallback turn.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout at PR head `c4f5337a7fdcc9f11c17ff6f42d8e8295fb77eed`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/home`, isolated workspace `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/workspace`, and a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1`. No real user session, live channel, or production OpenClaw home was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Configured `agents.defaults.model.primary` as `primary-local/primary-model` with `fallbacks: ["fallback-local/fallback-model"]`.
2. Started a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` server that returns four HTTP 429 responses for `primary-model`, then returns streaming success for `fallback-model`; after that, `primary-model` returns streaming success.
3. Ran two local embedded agent turns against the same session id:

`/usr/bin/node openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id pr75270-proof --thinking off --json --timeout 120 --message <proof message>`

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from `/var/tmp/openclaw-pr75270-proof/proof-output.json`:

```json
{
  "first": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 fallback proof OK",
    "provider": "fallback-local",
    "model": "fallback-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [
      {
        "provider": "primary-local",
        "model": "primary-model",
        "error": "429 proof primary rate limit",
        "reason": "rate_limit",
        "status": 429
      }
    ],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model",
      "fallback-model",
      "fallback-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "fallback-local",
      "model": "fallback-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "second": {
    "exitCode": 0,
    "text": "PR75270 primary recovered OK",
    "provider": "primary-local",
    "model": "primary-model",
    "fallbackAttempts": [],
    "requestModels": [
      "primary-model",
      "primary-model"
    ],
    "storeAfterTurn": {
      "modelProvider": "primary-local",
      "model": "primary-model",
      "providerOverride": null,
      "modelOverride": null,
      "modelOverrideSource": null,
      "fallbackNoticeSelectedModel": null,
      "fallbackNoticeActiveModel": null
    }
  },
  "allRequestModels": [
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "fallback-model",
    "primary-model",
    "primary-model"
  ]
}
```

The runtime log also showed the fallback decision on the first turn:

```text
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=primary-local/primary-model reason=rate_limit next=fallback-local/fallback-model detail=429 proof primary rate limit
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=primary-local/primary-model candidate=fallback-local/fallback-model reason=unknown next=none
```

Observed result after fix: The first turn used the configured fallback after the synthetic primary returned 429, and `fallbackAttempts` recorded the primary failure. The same session's persisted auto override fields were clear after the fallback turn. The second turn, with the primary endpoint recovered, requested and completed on `primary-local/primary-model` with no fallback attempts. This verifies the session did not stay pinned to the fallback model.

What was not tested: This proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint rather than a paid hosted provider, did not exercise real Telegram/WhatsApp delivery, and did not test provider-specific OAuth rotation. Those paths are covered separately by the automated tests listed above.

target.title: feat(agents): add description field for dynamic agent discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Surfaces configured agent descriptions through `agents_list` so orchestrator agents can understand sub-agent capabilities at runtime.

This was originally requested in #7490. Current `main` already accepts `description` in agent config; this PR completes the runtime/tooling behavior by returning nonblank descriptions from `agents_list` and omitting blank configured values.

## Changes

- Update `agents_list` tool output to include trimmed descriptions when set
- Omit blank configured descriptions from returned agent entries
- Update `agents_list` tool description and docs to reflect the field
- Add focused tests for configured nonblank descriptions and blank omission
- Refresh prompt snapshots / generated config docs baseline as needed

## Use Case

In multi-agent setups, orchestrators need to route tasks to the right sub-agent without hardcoding catalogs. With this change:

```yaml
agents:
  list:
    - id: analyzer
      name: Data Analyzer
      description: 'Statistical analysis, chart generation, anomaly detection'
    - id: writer
      name: Report Writer
      description: 'Summarization, formatting, document generation'
```

`agents_list` returns:
```json
{
  "agents": [
    { "id": "analyzer", "name": "Data Analyzer", "description": "Statistical analysis, chart generation, anomaly detection", "configured": true },
    { "id": "writer", "name": "Report Writer", "description": "Summarization, formatting, document generation", "configured": true }
  ]
}
```

This is fully backward compatible — existing configs without `description` are unaffected.

Closes #7490

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `agents_list` now returns configured nonblank `agents.list[].description` values and omits blank configured descriptions.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway built from this PR branch at head `ea4113c4c2`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr75225-proof`, loopback Gateway `127.0.0.1:19977`, plugins disabled, no production Shannon runtime touched.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr75225-proof \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr75225-proof/.openclaw \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr75225-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
node openclaw.mjs --log-level info gateway --port 19977 --bind loopback

curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19977/tools/invoke \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'x-openclaw-scopes: operator.write' \
  -d '{"tool":"agents_list","action":"json","args":{},"sessionKey":"agent:main:proof"}'
```

  Proof config: requester `main` has `subagents.allowAgents: ["*"]`; `main` and `worker` have nonblank descriptions; `blank` has `description: "   "`.

- Evidence after fix: copied live output from the real Gateway `/tools/invoke` response:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "result": {
    "details": {
      "requester": "main",
      "allowAny": true,
      "agents": [
        {
          "id": "main",
          "name": "Main Proof",
          "configured": true,
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex", "source": "implicit" },
          "description": "Primary proof agent description should appear in agents_list."
        },
        {
          "id": "blank",
          "name": "Blank Proof",
          "configured": true,
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex", "source": "implicit" }
        },
        {
          "id": "worker",
          "name": "Worker Proof",
          "configured": true,
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex", "source": "implicit" },
          "description": "Handles focused backend proof tasks."
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: nonblank descriptions are present for `main` and `worker`; the blank configured description for `blank` is omitted from the returned entry.
- What was not tested: multi-host or production Shannon runtime behavior was not tested; the proof intentionally used an isolated loopback Gateway so no production OpenClaw runtime or secrets were touched.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a local Gateway proof, not a full fleet proof. It directly exercises the changed `agents_list` runtime path through the Gateway tool invocation.

## Tests and validation

Additional validation on the same rebased head:

```sh
pnpm build
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.agents.test.ts src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.test.ts docs/tools/subagents.md docs/gateway/config-agents.md
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/openclaw-tools.agents.test.ts src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.test.ts
pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
pnpm config:docs:check
```

Results: build passed; format passed; focused Vitest passed (4 files / 28 tests); prompt snapshots current (7 files); config docs baseline OK.

target.title: fix: CLI auth status/probe path regression
target.body:
## Summary

Found one regression in the CLI auth status/probe path. The commit intentionally makes unscoped external CLI discovery skip missing runtime-only profiles, but `models status` and `models status --probe` still load the auth store without passing the providers they are about to report or probe.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Medium: `models status --probe` misses CLI-only credentials after scoped discovery** (regression)
  - File: `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts:259`
  - Evidence: `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts:210` now returns false for unscoped missing external CLI profiles unless a matching stored OAuth profile already exists. `buildProbeTargets` still calls `ensureAuthProfileStore(agentDir)` without `allowKeychainPrompt: false`, `externalCliProviderIds`, or `externalCliProfileIds`, even though the function receives the exact `providers` and probe profile filters at `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts:254-256`. A focused smoke with a temporary Codex CLI auth file confirmed `resolveExternalCliAuthProfiles(emptyStore)` returns `[]`, while `resolveExternalCliAuthProfiles(emptyStore, { providerIds: ["openai-codex"] })` returns `["openai-codex:default"]`.
  - Impact: A user who only has file-backed Codex/Claude/Minimax CLI credentials and no persisted OpenClaw auth profile can resolve runtime auth through the newly scoped model-auth path, but `openclaw models status --probe` will build no profile target for that same credential. This produces false “nothing to probe” or missing-auth diagnostics for the provider most likely being checked.
  - Suggested fix: In `buildProbeTargets`, load the store with a scope derived from the requested probe surface, for example `ensureAuthProfileStore(agentDir, { allowKeychainPrompt: false, config: cfg, externalCliProviderIds: providers, externalCliProfileIds: options.profileIds })`. The plain status path at `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts:223` should likewise pass the config-derived external CLI scope, matching `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts:296-301`.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `src/commands/models/list.probe.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-scope.ts`
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`
- `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `src/agents/model-auth.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12
- Latest main at intake: 581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12
- Original commit author: Peter Steinberger
- GitHub author: @steipete
- Highest severity: medium
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `54e6e3d7daf5d0d857edf756b35628a29d11c7f5..581fbea1d65355917886babffe4161f8b3178a12`
- Changed files: `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-scope.ts`, `src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth.ts`
- Code read: changed files, auth store overlay path, external auth overlay, auth profile order, CLI model status/probe callers, gateway auth-status caller
- Dependencies/web: no dependency changes; no web lookup needed

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- Focused external CLI resolver smoke with temporary Codex CLI auth file
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-sync.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.external-cli-scope.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/commands/models/list.probe.targets.test.ts`

Known review limits:

target.title: fix(whatsapp): reuse active QR and preserve runtime warnings
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: even after the QR preflight contract, WhatsApp still had two remaining coherence gaps: the gateway could ignore an already-active QR until after preflight, and runtime warnings could disappear when linked state was temporarily unknown.
- Why it matters: users could get an unnecessary unstable-auth result instead of reusing a valid QR, and operational status could lose important reconnect/logged-out signals during auth-stabilizing windows.
- What changed: this PR adds an optional existing-QR hook ahead of preflight, uses it in WhatsApp, and restores runtime/session warnings when linked state is unknown.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not broaden QR behavior to other channels beyond the new optional hook; only WhatsApp implements it here.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #67815
- Related #67818
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the gateway QR flow still had no pre-preflight “existing result” seam, and WhatsApp runtime warning collection still over-relied on a known linked bit.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no optional `loginWithQrStartExisting` hook in the shared gateway adapter, and no focused regression coverage for runtime warnings under unknown linked state.
- Contributing context (if known): this is the last QR/runtime coherence follow-up after #67815 and the QR preflight PR.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/login-qr.test.ts`
  - `extensions/whatsapp/src/status-issues.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/web.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: gateway login returns an existing QR before preflight rejection, and runtime/session warnings still surface when linked state is unknown.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the remaining behavior is isolated to the WhatsApp QR/runtime warning seam and the shared gateway login ordering.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): #67818 covers the QR preflight/result-code layer.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `web.login.start` can reuse an already-active WhatsApp QR before preflight rejects the attempt.
- WhatsApp runtime/session warnings still show up when linked state is temporarily unknown.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[existing QR] -> [gateway preflight timeout] -> [unstable result]
[linked unknown] -> [status collector] -> [missing runtime warning]

After:
[existing QR] -> [existing-result hook] -> [reuse QR]
[linked unknown] -> [status collector] -> [runtime/session warning still visible]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp QR login and status surfaces
- Relevant config (redacted): default WhatsApp account config

### Steps

1. Keep an existing WhatsApp QR active, then trigger `web.login.start` during an auth-stabilizing window.
2. Exercise WhatsApp status collection while linked state is unknown but runtime/session state is still present.
3. Verify the existing QR is reused first and runtime warnings still surface.

### Expected

- Existing QR is returned before preflight rejection.
- Runtime/session warnings remain visible when linked state is unknown.

### Actual

- Verified with focused WhatsApp login/status and gateway coverage after the change.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/login-qr.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/status-issues.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/web.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- Edge cases checked:
  - existing QR reuse before preflight
  - runtime warnings with unknown linked state
  - Plugin SDK baseline drift for the optional existing-result hook
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live WhatsApp QR/device behavior outside automated coverage

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the shared gateway adapter grows another optional QR hook.
  - Mitigation: it is optional, only WhatsApp implements it here, and the Plugin SDK baseline plus focused tests lock the contract in.

target.title: feat(web-fetch): add Tavily as a native `web_fetch` provider
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Tavily ships as a `web_search` provider and exposes `tavily_extract` as a tool, but `tools.web.fetch` only auto-detects Firecrawl. Users with a Tavily key have no first-class `web_fetch` path.
- Why it matters: parity with the Tavily `web_search` story and with Firecrawl's two-key model. One Tavily account covers `/search` and `/extract`, so onboarding shouldn't ask for a second key.
- What changed: register `createTavilyWebFetchProvider` (auto-detect order `70`, after Firecrawl at `50`) backed by `runTavilyExtract`; declare `contracts.webFetchProviders: ["tavily"]` and add `webFetch.apiKey`/`webFetch.baseUrl` config; resolve credentials from `webFetch.apiKey` → `webSearch.apiKey` → `TAVILY_API_KEY`; 
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no Tavily-only knobs added to `web_fetch` (`query`, `chunks_per_source`, `include_images`, `include_favicon`, `timeout`, `include_usage`, multi-URL stay on `tavily_extract`); no changes to the Firecrawl provider, the `tavily_search`/`tavily_extract` tools, or the `web_fetch` contract/SDK shape.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New bundled `web_fetch` provider id `tavily`, selectable via `tools.web.fetch.provider: "tavily"` or auto-detected when only `TAVILY_API_KEY` (or `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`) is set. Firecrawl wins auto-detect when both are configured (order 50 vs 70).
- New optional config: `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webFetch.apiKey` (override) and `webFetch.baseUrl`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 25.3.0 (darwin)
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A (provider plumbing only)
- Integration/channel: Tavily API (`api.tavily.com`)
- Relevant config (redacted):
```json5
{
   "plugins":{
      "entries":{
         "tavily":{
            "enabled":true,
            "config":{
               "webSearch":{
                  "apiKey":"tvly-***"
               }
            }
         }
      }
   },
   "tools":{
      "web":{
         "fetch":{
            "provider":"tavily"
         }
      }
   }
}
```

### Steps

1. Set `TAVILY_API_KEY` (or `plugins.entries.tavily.config.webSearch.apiKey`).
2. Configure `tools.web.fetch.provider: "tavily"`.
3. Invoke `web_fetch` with a URL

### Expected

- Tavily provider is selected; `/extract` is called once.
- Result honors `maxChars` truncation on `rawContent` and `content`.

### Actual

- Matches expected. With both Firecrawl and Tavily keys set, auto-detect picks Firecrawl first (50 < 70).

## Evidence

target.title: fix(agents): honor skipBootstrap at runtime injection path (#75184)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` was advertised as a way to skip workspace bootstrap content, but it was a no-op for the runtime system-prompt injection. `systemPromptReport` still showed AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md, HEARTBEAT.md, BOOTSTRAP.md injected with their full per-file content.
- **Root cause:** the flag was checked at workspace-creation time (`agent-command.ts:375 -> ensureBootstrapFiles: !agentCfg?.skipBootstrap`) but the runtime injection path `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` (and `resolveBootstrapContextForRun` which wraps it) loaded the workspace bootstrap files unconditionally. So files created during a previous bootstrap kept being injected even after the operator set the flag.
- **What changed:** added an early return in `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` when `config.agents.defaults.skipBootstrap === true`. Empty bootstrap files → empty contextFiles → no injection. Workspace-creation behavior unchanged.

## Linked Issue

- Closes #75184
- [x] Bug fix

## Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core` clean
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #75184. `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` correctly suppressed workspace AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md bootstrap-file injection, but the early-return that implemented the skip also bypassed `applyBootstrapHookOverrides`, so `agent:bootstrap` hooks (including the bundled `bootstrap-extra-files` handler at `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.ts:25`) no longer ran. Operators who set `skipBootstrap: true` to opt out of workspace bootstrap, while still relying on hook-provided context (custom workflow rules, organization guardrails), saw those hook-injected files silently disappear from the agent's runtime prompt. martingarramon reviewed and confirmed: "The short-circuit before the workspace load while keeping `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` in the path is the right fix for the codex-bot follow-up about hook bypass, and the second test (`hook-injected files still appear when skipBootstrap is true`) covers it."
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `b466f6065e` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun(...)` resolver (`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`) through the real hook-registry path with `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` and a registered `agent:bootstrap` hook that injects an additional bootstrap file via `applyBootstrapHookOverrides`. No mocks of the resolver under test or the hook-override application.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "skipBootstrap"` from the repository root, which compiles the production source via tsgo and drives the live resolver against a real hook registry.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |agents| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts > resolveBootstrapFilesForRun > returns no bootstrap files when agents.defaults.skipBootstrap is true even if workspace files exist 8ms
   ✓ |agents| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts > resolveBootstrapFilesForRun > still runs agent:bootstrap hooks when skipBootstrap is true so hook-injected files reach the prompt 1ms
   ✓ |agents| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts > resolveBootstrapFilesForRun > still resolves workspace bootstrap files when skipBootstrap is false or unset 3ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  3 passed | 27 skipped (30)
     Start at  11:51:15
     Duration  265ms

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 2.18s
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver run: (a) with `skipBootstrap: true` and workspace AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md present on disk, the resolved bootstrap-files array is empty for the workspace portion; (b) with `skipBootstrap: true` AND a registered `agent:bootstrap` hook that injects an additional file, the hook-injected file appears in the result — proving `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` runs in both branches; (c) with `skipBootstrap: false`, workspace bootstrap content resolves normally.
- **Observed result after fix:** Operators who set `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` still get workspace AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md suppressed, but their hook-injected `bootstrap-extra-files` content correctly appears in the agent's runtime prompt. Pre-fix on the same install, hook-provided context silently disappeared.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end agent prompt assembly against a live model — the resolver test exercises the bootstrap-files resolution surface directly, which is the contract `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` controls; downstream prompt assembly consumes the resolver output unchanged.
